title: lastrea pubescens
intro: Lastrea pubescens, also known as the pubescent lady fern, is a species of fern native to tropical Asia, characterized by its delicate fronds and distinctive hairy stems. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antifungal activities. The health benefits associated with Lastrea pubescens include the relief of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and reduce the risk of cardiovascular disease. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being dried and made into capsules or tablets for oral consumption.
